revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Iyadani-ji]][[ja:弥谷寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Iyadaniji 04.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Mitoyo|qid=Q873578|1=Mitoyo, Kagawa}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.229722222222|N|133.72426111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|part_of={{ill|Shikoku Pilgrimage|qid=Q1972399|1=Shikoku Pilgrimage}}}}
{{Japanese temple |Name = Iyadan-ji |image = Iyadanizi0ɨ.jpg |imageSize = 250 |imageCaption = Main Hall |Location = {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|qid=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|Mitoyo, Kagawa|en|Mitoyo, Kagawa|qid=Q873578|lt=Mitoyo|1=Mitoyo, Kagawa}}{{ill|Mino Town, Kagawa|ja|三野町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Town, Kagawa|1=Q16768898|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16768898}} Ōmi Otsu 70 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|13|47|N|133|43|27.34|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Iyadan-ji}} |Mountain = Kengo-san |TempleInstitute = {{ill|Senju-in|ja|千手院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senju-in|1=Senju-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11405520}} |Sect = {{ill|Shingon Zentsūji School|ja|真言宗善通寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Zentsūji School|1=Shingon Zentsuji Sect|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11583514}} |TempleType = Grand Head Temple |Principalimage = {{ill|Senju Kannon|ja|千手観音|1=Thousand-armed Kannon|2=ja|3=千手観音|lt=Thousand-Armed Kannon|4=fr|5=Thousand-armed Kannon|6=ko|7=관세음보살|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senju Kannon|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |FoundedYear = (Traditionally attributed to the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}) |FoundedBy = (Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|qid=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki|1=Gyōki}} and {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|qid=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu|1=Emperor Shōmu}}) |TempleRank = 71st Temple of {{ill|Shikoku Pilgrimage|en|Shikoku Pilgrimage|qid=Q1972399|lt=Shikoku Pilgrimage|1=Shikoku Pilgrimage}} |CulturalAssets = {{Plainlist| * Gilt bronze five-pronged vajra bell of the Four Heavenly Kings (National Important Cultural Property) * ''Butsusetsu Kanbutsu Sanmaikai Kyō'' (Prefectural Designated Tangible Cultural Property) * Iyadan-ji Faith Sites (Prefectural Historic Site)}} |OfficialWebsite = https://iyadanizi.xsrv.jp }}
'''Iyadan-ji''' (弥谷寺) is a grand head temple of the {{ill|Shingon Zentsūji School|ja|真言宗善通寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Zentsūji School|1=Shingon Zentsuji Sect|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11583514}} located in {{ill|Mitoyo, Kagawa|en|Mitoyo, Kagawa|qid=Q873578|lt=Mitoyo|1=Mitoyo, Kagawa}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|qid=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. The temple is also known by its mountain name, Kengo-san (剣五山), and its temple institute name, Senju-in (千手院). The principal image is {{ill|Senju Kannon|ja|千手観音|1=Thousand-armed Kannon|2=ja|3=千手観音|lt=Thousand-Armed Kannon|4=fr|5=Thousand-armed Kannon|6=ko|7=관세음보살|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senju Kannon|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is the 71st temple of the {{ill|Shikoku Pilgrimage|en|Shikoku Pilgrimage|qid=Q1972399|lt=Shikoku Pilgrimage|1=Shikoku Pilgrimage}}. * Principal mantra: "On Basara Taramaki Rik." * {{ill|Goeika|ja|ご詠歌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goeika|1=eika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11267316}}: "Iyadan-ji where even sinners are welcomed; as fleeting as this world may be, good friends make it better." * Stamps: Principal image seal, Oku-no-in academic site seal, Seven Sites Pilgrimage seal{{efn|The Seven Sites Pilgrimage around Temples 71–77 near Zentsūji was performed from the Edo period onward.}}
== Overview == The main hall of Iyadan-ji is located about 225 meters above sea level, midway up Mount Iyadani (382 meters). Behind the main hall is a rock cavity where the original Senju Kannon statue was enshrined. The entire mountain is considered sacred, and the temple is said to be one of Japan's three great spiritual sites, alongside {{ill|Mount Osore|en|Mount Osore|qid=Q1377333|lt=Mount Osore|1=Mount Osore}} and {{ill|Usuki Stone Buddhas|en|Usuki Stone Buddhas|qid=Q2921458|lt=Usuki Stone Buddhas|1=Usuki Stone Buddhas}}.{{efn|Some claim other spiritual sites are included among the three.}}
The surrounding area of Mount Iyadani is designated as the Iyadani Mountain Natural Environmental Conservation Area.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.kagawa.lg.jp/documents/10786/1.pdf|title=Iyadani Mountain Natural Environmental Conservation Area |publisher=Kagawa Prefecture |accessdate=2024-11-21}}</ref>
== History == According to temple tradition, the temple was established under the imperial wish of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|qid=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu|1=Emperor Shōmu}}, with {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|qid=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki|1=Gyōki}} constructing the buildings to memorialize the repose of {{ill|Empress Kōmyō|en|Empress Kōmyō|qid=Q837932|lt=Empress Kōmyō|1=Empress Kōmyō}}, enshrining a copy of the ''Avatamsaka Sutra'' believed to have been transcribed by the empress. Initially named Lotus Mountain Hakokoku-ji, it was said to offer views of the eight provinces of China and Shikoku.
During his childhood (ages 7–12), {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|qid=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}} reportedly studied diligently in a cave at this site known as Lion’s Cave (Shishino-iwaya). After returning from China in 807 (Daido 2), Kūkai revisited the cave to perform a 1,000-seat goma fire ritual, during which he had a vision of {{ill|Zaō Gongen|lt=Zaō Gongen|lt=Zao Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Gongen|qq=}}. Following this, he enshrined a {{ill|Senju Kannon|ja|千手観音|1=Thousand-armed Kannon|2=ja|3=千手観音|lt=Thousand-Armed Kannon|4=fr|5=Thousand-armed Kannon|6=ko|7=관세음보살|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senju Kannon|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} statue and renamed the mountain Kengo-san ("Mountain of Swords"), also associating the area with "Miya-dani," meaning "Buddha’s Valley," derived from Miya-san, the Buddha’s dwelling.
== Cultural Assets == ; National Important Cultural Properties * Gilt bronze five-pronged vajra bell of the Four Heavenly Kings (attributed to Kūkai, brought from Tang China) - Designated March 27, 1901.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index|title=Database of Cultural Properties |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
; Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties * ''Butsusetsu Kanbutsu Sanmaikai Kyō'' - Designated March 30, 2007.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.kagawa.lg.jp/kenkyoui/shogaigakushu/bunkazai/culturalassets/culturalassets_5.html|title=Kagawa Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties |publisher=Kagawa Prefectural Board of Education}}</ref>
; Prefectural Historic Sites * Iyadan-ji Faith Sites - Designated June 4, 1968.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.kagawa.lg.jp/kenkyoui/shogaigakushu/bunkazai/culturalassets/culturalassets_12.html|title=Kagawa Prefectural Historic Sites |publisher=Kagawa Prefectural Board of Education}}</ref>
== Access == ; By Train * {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|en|Shikoku Railway Company|qid=Q496531|lt=Shikoku Railway Company|1=Shikoku Railway Company}} Yosan Line – 3.3 km from {{ill|Mino Station|en|Mino Station|qid=Q5358745|lt=Mino Station (Kagawa)|1=Mino Station}}. ; By Road * Prefectural Route 48 – 1.3 km to the parking area.
== Adjacent Temples == ; {{ill|Shikoku Pilgrimage|en|Shikoku Pilgrimage|qid=Q1972399|lt=Shikoku Pilgrimage|1=Shikoku Pilgrimage}} : 70 {{ill|Motoyama-ji|ja|本山寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoyama-ji|1=Honzan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11520182}} --{{small|(11.3 km)}}-* '''71 Iyadan-ji''' --{{small|(3.5 km)}}-- 72 {{ill|Mandaraji|ja|曼荼羅寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mandaraji|qq=}}
== Notes == {{Notelist}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://iyadanizi.xsrv.jp Official Website of Iyadan-ji]
